component: Header asset-card navigation (.hdr-cards: hero + 5 asset cards)
issue: The 5 asset nav cards (Indian/FD/MF/US/Trading) do not fit; rightmost cards (US, Trading) escape the viewport by up to 264px and are clipped, and card labels truncate ('INDIAN EQUIT…'). Only ~4 of the cards are reachable without horizontal scrolling.
rootCause: grid-template-columns: repeat(5, minmax(130px,1fr)) → 650px hard minimum; the <=1080px rule switches the row to overflow-x:auto (horizontal scroll) instead of wrapping/collapsing, so primary navigation is partially hidden and offered only via an unindicated side-scroll.
recommendedFix: Below ~1080px wrap the cards (grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px,1fr))) or collapse to a 2-row / 2-3 col grid; if keeping horizontal scroll, add a visible scroll affordance (edge fade + scroll-snap) and never truncate the active label.

issue: Dense holdings tables clip their right-hand columns at tablet (P&L / Return% / Day% off-screen, e.g. '₹19,7…' truncated). FDTab table has minWidth:760. Under long-content stress the Indian table breaks out of .ovx and overflows the whole document by 158px.
rootCause: Wide fixed-column tables inside a narrow .ovx (overflow-x:auto) card with no column-priority/stacking strategy on tablet; the scroll affordance is invisible so hidden columns read as missing data. Long unbreakable cell content escapes .ovx entirely.
recommendedFix: Add a visible horizontal-scroll affordance to .ovx (edge shadow/fade), and on tablet either freeze the first column + scroll, or hide low-priority columns (Return%/Day%) behind a toggle. Add overflow-wrap/min-width:0 on cells to keep long content inside .ovx.

issue: No horizontal scrollbar appears anywhere in the matrix (0/176 cells) NOT because content fits, but because html{overflow-x:hidden} silently CLIPS the overflow. RSP-001/002/004 are therefore invisible to a casual look while their content is lost off the right edge.
rootCause: overflow-x:hidden is used as a global band-aid that suppresses the SYMPTOM (scrollbar) of unresolved child overflow, converting it into silent content clipping. It also defeats horizontal-scroll affordances on legitimately scrollable children.
recommendedFix: Treat overflow-x:hidden as a backstop only AFTER the child-level overflows (RSP-001/002/004) are fixed. Audit with it temporarily removed to surface every offender; keep it only on a dedicated wrapper, not on html, so intended scroll containers still work.

route: overview, indian, fd, mf, us, algo (summary/KPI cards)
component: Summary cards (.csm .lbl/.vmd/.sub/.rs) and header card subtitles (.hdr-card .sub)
issue: Card values (.vmd, escape ≤249px), labels (.lbl ≤249px), subtitles (.sub ≤274px) and the ₹-glyph span (.rs ≤189px) overflow their cards at tablet and are clipped. .sub is also the #1 offender under browser zoom.
rootCause: Fixed/min card widths + white-space:nowrap values + clamp() fonts that bottom out at their floor on narrow screens, so the text can't shrink and escapes; overflow:hidden then clips it without an ellipsis cue in several cases.
recommendedFix: Add min-width:0 to flex/grid card children, allow value wrapping or fluid down-scaling (lower clamp floor), and ensure text-overflow:ellipsis where truncation is acceptable. Verify .g3/.g4 collapse to fewer columns earlier (they currently rely on the tab-level grids).

issue: At 150% zoom the document gains 177px of horizontal scroll; at 200% it gains 715px. Content requires two-dimensional scrolling. div.sub is the top offender at every zoom level.
rootCause: Fixed pixel widths and min-widths do not reflow under zoom (zoom shrinks the effective CSS viewport); the same fixed-card / fixed-label issues as RSP-001/004 surface as horizontal scroll. Borderline WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.10 (Reflow) concern.
recommendedFix: Replace fixed px min-widths on cards/labels/nav with fluid (%/fr/minmax(0,...)) units and ch-based caps; retest at 200% (≈640px effective) — target zero horizontal scroll, matching the 1.4.10 reflow bar.

breakpoint: all (more items off-screen at wider viewports)
issue: The ticker parks duplicated marquee content thousands of px off-screen (escapeR up to ~23,700px) — this is the sole reason the Macro tab reports 200–340 'offenders' per cell. It is masked by .tkv{overflow:hidden} + -webkit-mask-image, so it is NOT a visible layout bug.
rootCause: Standard duplicated-track marquee; the off-screen half is intentional. Risk is only the mask-image fade having no @supports fallback (older browsers show a hard clip instead of a fade).
recommendedFix: No layout change needed. Optionally add an @supports (mask-image) fallback and prefers-reduced-motion already pauses it (globals.css:624). Documented here so the offender counts are not misread as 300 bugs.

issue: Fixed-px labels crush their adjacent bars on narrow cards, and two grids have responsive gaps: .pjx-gcards stays 5-col with no rule between 760 and 1000px (cards collapse to ~137px), .ptorn keeps a 150px label with no rule between 561-759px.
rootCause: Fixed px label columns + missing intermediate breakpoints. Mostly desktop-safe; bites in the named tablet/large-phone windows.
recommendedFix: Convert fixed label widths to minmax(0, max-content)/clamp and add the missing 760-999px (growth cards → 2-3 col) and 561-759px (tornado label shrink) breakpoints.

component: Raw px font sizes bypassing --fs-* tokens (.chip 12px, .ecal-i 11px)
issue: Two font sizes are hardcoded in px instead of the --fs-* scale, so they do not track the root clamp() and read oversized/undersized relative to neighbours when the root font scales.
rootCause: Project convention violation (sizes must come from --fs-* tokens in globals.css).
recommendedFix: Replace 12px → var(--fs-2xs)/var(--fs-xs) and 11px → var(--fs-2xs) to restore token-driven scaling.

issue: max-width:1560px holds from 1080px all the way to 2100px, leaving ~180px gutters each side at 1920px and large empty margins — wasted whitespace (not broken). No layout damage; purely density.
rootCause: 1020px-wide breakpoint gap in the max-width ladder (1560 → 1840@2100 → 2560@3200).
recommendedFix: Optional: add a 1600-1900px step (e.g. max-width:1680-1720px) or let the grid breathe with a fluid clamp() max-width if denser wide layouts are desired.

issue: Sticky headers rely on backdrop-filter: blur(12px) with no solid-colour @supports fallback; on browsers without backdrop-filter the header becomes transparent and rows bleed through illegibly while scrolling.
rootCause: Frosted-glass sticky header without a graceful-degradation background.
recommendedFix: Add @supports not (backdrop-filter: blur(1px)) { .ovx thead th { background: var(--thead-bg-solid); } } or set an opaque base colour under the blur.

breakpoint: 861-920 (between the only macro breakpoint at 920 and the desktop layout)
issue: The macro grid jumps from 3-column (50:50 split) straight to a full single-column stack at 920px with no intermediate step; in the 861-919px window the left two columns are ~25% (~215-225px) and cramped before they collapse.
rootCause: A single 920px breakpoint for a 3-region grid; no 2-column intermediate.
recommendedFix: Optional intermediate step ~720-920px: drop hot-sectors below and run sentiment | (movers+calendar) two-up, or stack at a higher threshold so the cramped 861-919px window is avoided.

issue: The deployment sparkline height is hardcoded at 110px; width is responsive (ResizeObserver) but the chart does not scale vertically, so its aspect ratio distorts on very narrow cards.
rootCause: Fixed pixel SVG height instead of an aspect-ratio or viewBox-driven height.
recommendedFix: Drive height from a viewBox + width:100% (as the other 6 SVG charts do) or an aspect-ratio, so it scales proportionally.
